summary.professional_summary: I have 10 + Years IT of experience in DWH using informatica tool in 6+ Year worked Development, Enhancement and ETL testing resolutions projects. Experience in created mappings using needed Transformations in the informatica tool 4 years on words Hanover insurance project working as ETL MIS Batch monitoring, Production issues resolutions and IBM IGC web-based tool 1. Oracle , 2.SQL server, 3.DB2, 4. Hadoop data bases refreshing, data bases renewal process and Hadoop certificate setup process. Good experience in the ETL process to Extract, Transform and Load the data into stage area and data warehouse. Preparing the Documentation for the mappings according to the Business Requirements. Good knowledge in Data warehousing concepts like Star Schema, Dimensions and Fact tables. Optimizing Informatica Mappings and Sessions to improve the performance. Extensively used Informatica client tools – Source Analyzer, Target Designer, Mapping designer, Mapplet Designer, Transformation developer, Repository Manager and Workflow Manager Extensively used Visual Studio tool – Packages in SSIS and Cubes in SSAS. Possess complete domain and development Life Cycle knowledge of Data Warehousing. Experience in the SQL and PL/SQL and SQL Server trained on JAVA/J2EE, SAP BODI/BODS ODI and Data stage. Excellent problem-solving skills with a strong technical background and good interpersonal skills. Quick learner and excellent team player, ability to meet deadlines and work under pressure. Baseline work is typically the work needed to keep our systems up and running. It includes production support, system maintenance, compliance, rate changes, ad hoc reporting, etc. Baseline work is typically short in duration. The HTG Development and BA/QA Baseline support groups are staffed to only support small projects. Most projects are less than 100 hours. Project exceeding 300 hours would need to be assessed to determine if they should be funded and approved as enhancements. a. Nightly Cycle Support, b. Daytime on line system Support, c. Release Management activities -Production change, Business change, Technical support ,Miscellaneous .  Environment Informatica 10.5.1, Toad 12X, WinSCP5X,Putty,IBM Workload Scheduler, ,IBM Infosphere Information Governance Catalog, Informatica Metaquery 10.2  Duration From (mm/yy) 03/2021 To (mm/yy) tilldate  Role / Responsibility System Integration Specialist  Contributions As part of the MIS base line team member - Worked on the Incidents, Demands, Technology place requests, Production issues. Informatica code fix in the Development and moved the code to Production env with the help of PowerCenter Movers team. IBM Infosphere Information Governance Catalog (lxpr01igc & lxut01igc ) server issues resolutions - Migrated data bases from old to New -HOMSQLSPPR06A - PLUS_prd, HOMSQLSPPR06A – BonDirectprd ,HOMSQLPLPR05A – PEGAEFOPROD, BillingCenterV10_prd,ClaimCenter_V10_prd,Oracle - MISPPR2 ,Oracle – MISPPR2 and new user access validation and providing the right roles. Knox certificate renewal process implementation process -every yearly once activity, Monthly database activities, Weekly activities, new terms import /export activities, version upgraded data bases connector problems solving, Agent not available issues resolutions, IBM IGC application disappear issues resolutions, enabling the server options/ properties issues resolutions  Team Size 10   Project Name Humana Health care & MKS Materials  Client US  Project Description Currently the OA-EDW (Staging) data is being loaded at every 30 minutes. The data from the Staging->EDW reporting tables occurs once in a day at the end of the business day for that day’s data. This data is getting pulled from the VIEW of the staging table. The EDW reporting tables are full loads (truncate and reload) on each run, which means the data in the staging table is loaded all over again on each run. This is because the prior day’s business may be received beyond business date +1 and may be resent on subsequent business dates. Based on recent discussion with business stakeholders, it was brought to our attention that the data to be truncated and reloaded will be around 200million records. During the full load on each run, the data will be unavailable to users until the processing is complete. This means the report (OA section of the report) will be unavailable. In order to make this data available continuously for the users, the logic needs to be changed from a truncate/reload to insert/update or incremental load. N/A  Environment Informatica 10.5.1, Toad 12X, WinSCP5X,Putty,Control -M, Autosys jobs ,IBM IGC tool,  Duration From (mm/yy) 03/2019 To (mm/yy) 2021  Role / Responsibility System Integration Specialist  Contributions The Detail Fact and the aggregate fact- both these tables are partitioned on MONTH/YEAR and type 1 slowly changing facts. (insert/Update) The business key is (Account/Department/Product/Entity WID/Transaction_DT) on the Detail fact (WC_PLANIT_ACTUALS_DAILY_F) The business key is (Metric_Name/ReportName/EntityWid/TransactionDT/CurrencyWId It is very important to understand that a group of accounts/Departments/Products for an Entity for a given transaction date will result in a metric value on the report. Since our job runs every 30 minutes there is the possibility for adjustments and new transactions coming for the same metric. So every time if there is an adjustment/New transaction- the records gets updated/Inserted in the source and we pick that row for processing. But when we calculate the metric value we need to factor all the related accounts/Departments/Product for that metric for a given transaction date. In other words if some rows in the same group are updated, we need to make sure we keep the entire set as one group when we aggregate the number for that metric irrespective of when the record came The data in the source table (STG.WC_OA_FMS_ACTUALS_DETAIL_STG) always persists. It’s an Insert/update type table. So historically data always resides in this table.  Team Size 5   NTT DATA GDS Project Name Apria Healthcare  Client US, Canada  Project Description There may be certain processes that are required to run every day. Due to up-stream issues, it may happen that the data wasn’t ready to be processed when the schedule kicked off the process. It may be the case the certain processes were turned off until the data was ready, and now that it is ready, we need to run those jobs (Suspended Billing is a good example). This section will cover setup and execution. We will assume that we are only working with the Daily schedule, but this process will work for all schedules.AS400 to UDB – Corporate/Branch AS400 systems as source to STGP01 db2 database in DWDB1 server to separate tables for each source systems. This is identical replication and Journal Sequence replication. There is no JDE control logic (since JDE SCP runs on weekly basis only) for Daily Load. Only the JDA Control logic is implemented for JDA Daily Load. The Job aborts when U_PROCESS_STATUS =’P’ in JDA Control table for the below 3 tables  Environment SQL Server 2012, Visual Studio-SSIS,SSAS, Winscp,AS400,Data stage,Aqtv9  Duration From (mm/yy) 03/2018 To (mm/yy) March/2019  Role / Responsibility System Integration Sr. Analyst  Contributions Creating Data Stage links, sessions, Parameter files and workflows using different stages to implement business logic. Preparation of Unit test cases also one of my responsibilities as per the business requirement. And also involved into Unit testing for the stage jobs developed by myself. Jobs Monitoring0100 PST PM Job (Daily Load-Once/Day-Late Night<--),0300 PST AM Job (0300 - Daily-Every Day-EDM Load Process<--),0600 PST AM Job (Daily Load-Once/Day<--) DataStage Server 9.1.2 for extraction, loading and transformation (ETL) of data in the data warehouse by applying the transform stage, filter stage, aggregator stage, remove duplicates stage, join stage, lookup stage etc., then we will take the help of DB team to fix it. Earlier some of the SSIS packages aborted in the production side code, we did analysis and provided root cause. Fast few weeks onwards we didn’t see any aborts SSIS packages, it might be stabilized. Still we are waiting for KT to do the development (Creating the stories, as part of SSIS packages) Incidents, As part of hourly fact table load process on 10v and 20V update fix: BPM task PID’s :-process instance & work item id’s status shows I_Completed or Assigned in the ORC schema ,so we need to updated the SQLSNAP_LOGMARKER to GETDATE() in the orc.dbo.BPM_WORK_ITEM table based on work item id or process instance id. Then see the status has changed 'Assigned' to 'Complete' in EDM.Fact. F_TASK table in the next run. Once found the status shows 'completed' in F_Task fact table, then we need closed the incident  Team Size 10  NTT DATA GDS Project Name Hilton, Loblaw (Planit, TMTP)  Client UK, Canada  Project Description TMTP reports are pushed to lobby 3 times in a day and the standard rooms available would get calculated based on available reservation data i.e. the reservation data gets loaded in EDW around 6 PM CST (from Monday to Friday and gets delayed over the weekend due to Weekly SRP load). The future Go Hilton report will be sourced from below data feeds. AS Forecast Files, CRS Snapshot Files, RAT Room type Inventory Files. First and Second TMTP Go Hilton reports would get published to lobby by considering the reservation data as on previous day. However, the last run of TMTP reports would consider the reservation data as on current day. eservation load is delayed over the weekend due to weekly SRP load. Hence, the standard rooms available in TMTP Go Hilton reports would get calculated based on the reservation data available in EDW as on last run.  Environment Informatica9.1.0/9.6.1,Toad11.6,Windows,Teradata,Winscp,SQL Server 2012, Visual Studio-SSIS,SSAS  Duration From (mm/yy) 05/2017 To (mm/yy) 02/2018  Role / Responsibility System Integration Sr. Analyst  Contributions Developing DDS and MT User list packages by using multiple transformations like Conditional Split, Data Conversion, Derived Column, Lookup, Row Count, Slowly Changing Dimension (SCD), Merge Join, Pivot and Bug fixing. Developing Stored Procedures and Views in SQL Server. Inspection, reviews and interaction with SQA for all deliverables I involved into the preparation of source to target mapping sheet which tell us what the source and target and which column is we need to map to target column and also what would be the business logic. Please pay attention to pre-sql in each session which is specific to a module. There is a delete statement to remove the data for the specific module and then load. There is no truncate while loading level0 tables to allow sharing of same table by all the modules. The Common_Cd_D dimension is used to store the surrogate keys for AVL_INFRA_CD_KY. This is done for performance reason and also to help in developing reports for different modules. Each data marts represent a collection of data pertaining to a single business process. In loan data marts BOM Involved in disbursal of loan amounts for various purposes like: Personal Loan, Educational Loan, Vehicle Loan, Housing Loan etc. The company requires different levels of analysis regarding loan amount, type of loans, type of customers, type of payment schedules, interest rates (variable or fixed), defaulters list and the penal interest calculations etc. They needed a Data Warehouse so that they could maintain historical data and central location for integration, analyze business in different locations, according profit areas, which could serve a purpose of a DSS for decision makers.  Environment Informatica 8.6.1, Oracle 9i ,Unix and TOAD  Duration From (mm/yy) 01/10 To (mm/yy) 04/11  Role / Responsibility Software Engineer  Contributions Gathering of user requirements and source system analysis and establishing mappings between sources and targets. It offers high quality new parts at the lowest wholesale prices to the public and dealers. Under this Data Mart data are captured related to Raw Materials and the processes that may affect the cost of materials procurement, storage, maintenance and dispatch. Main emphasis on analyzing those factors which affects directly the costing of materials for example: Materials procurement time, transportation cost, cost variance over the period of time, interest gain on capital blocked into materials, wastage ratio over the period of time, etc. Data for this Data Mart are captured from different Transactional Databases like: Purchase Order System, Materials Receipt System, Material Issue System, Inventory Control System, Material Costing System, etc.  Environment Informatica 8.6.1, Oracle 9i ,Unix and TOAD  Duration From (mm/yy) 11/08 To (mm/yy) 12/09  Role / Responsibility ETL Developer  Contributions Designed the Mapping Technical Specifications on the basis of Functional Requirements. Baseline work is typically the work needed to keep our systems up and running.  It includes production support, system maintenance, compliance, rate changes, ad hoc reporting, etc.  Baseline work is typically short in duration. The HTG Development and BA/QA Baseline support groups are staffed to only support small projects.  Most projects are less than 100 hours.  Project exceeding 300 hours would need to be assessed to determine if they should be funded and approved as enhancements. a. Nightly Cycle Support, b. Daytime on line system Support, c. Release Management activities -Production change, Business change, Technical support ,Miscellaneous .EnvironmentInformatica 10.5.1, Toad 12X, WinSCP5X,Putty,IBM Workload Scheduler,  ,IBM Infosphere Information Governance Catalog, Informatica Metaquery 10.2DurationFrom (mm/yy)03/2021To (mm/yy)tilldateRole / ResponsibilitySystem Integration Specialist ContributionsAs part of the MIS base line team member - Worked on the Incidents, Demands, Technology place requests, Production issues. Informatica code fix in the Development and moved the code to Production env with the help of PowerCenter Movers team.IBM Infosphere Information Governance Catalog (lxpr01igc & lxut01igc ) server issues resolutions - Migrated data bases from old to New -HOMSQLSPPR06A - PLUS_prd, HOMSQLSPPR06A – BonDirectprd ,HOMSQLPLPR05A – PEGAEFOPROD, BillingCenterV10_prd,ClaimCenter_V10_prd,Oracle - MISPPR2 ,Oracle – MISPPR2  and new user access validation and providing the right roles.Knox certificate renewal process implementation process -every yearly once activity, Monthly database activities, Weekly activities, new terms import /export activities, version upgraded data bases connector problems solving, Agent not available issues resolutions, IBM IGC application disappear issues resolutions, enabling the server options/ properties issues resolutionsTeam Size10Project NameHumana Health care & MKS  Materials ClientUSProject DescriptionCurrently the OA-EDW (Staging) data is being loaded at every 30 minutes. The data from the Staging->EDW reporting tables occurs once in a day at the end of the business day for that day’s data. This data is getting pulled from the VIEW of the staging table. The EDW reporting tables are full loads (truncate and reload) on each run, which means the data in the staging table is loaded all over again on each run. This is because the prior day’s business may be received beyond business date +1 and may be resent on subsequent business dates. Based on recent discussion with business stakeholders, it was brought to our attention that the data to be truncated and reloaded will be around 200million records. During the full load on each run, the data will be unavailable to users until the processing is complete. This means the report (OA section of the report) will be unavailable. In order to make this data available continuously for the users, the logic needs to be changed from a truncate/reload to insert/update or incremental load.N/AEnvironmentInformatica 10.5.1, Toad 12X, WinSCP5X,Putty,Control -M, Autosys jobs ,IBM IGC tool,  DurationFrom (mm/yy)03/2019To (mm/yy)2021Role / ResponsibilitySystem Integration Specialist ContributionsThe Detail Fact and the aggregate fact- both these tables are partitioned on MONTH/YEAR and type 1 slowly changing facts. (insert/Update)The business key is (Account/Department/Product/Entity WID/Transaction_DT) on the Detail fact (WC_PLANIT_ACTUALS_DAILY_F)The business key is (Metric_Name/ReportName/EntityWid/TransactionDT/CurrencyWIdIt is very important to understand that a group of accounts/Departments/Products for an Entity for a given transaction date will result in a metric value on the report.Since our job runs every 30 minutes there is the possibility for adjustments and new transactions coming for the same metric. So every time if there is an adjustment/New transaction- the records gets updated/Inserted in the source and we pick that row for processing. But when we calculate the metric value we need to factor all the related accounts/Departments/Product for that metric for a given transaction date. In other words if some rows in the same group are updated, we need to make sure we keep the entire set as one group when we aggregate the number for that metric irrespective of when the record came The data in the source table (STG.WC_OA_FMS_ACTUALS_DETAIL_STG) always persists. It’s an Insert/update type table. So historically data always resides in this table.Team Size5NTT DATA GDSProject NameApria Healthcare ClientUS, CanadaProject DescriptionThere may be certain processes that are required to run every day. It may be the case the certain processes were turned off until the data was ready, and now that it is ready, we need to run those jobs (Suspended Billing is a good example).  This section will cover setup and execution.  We will assume that we are only working with the Daily schedule, but this process will work for all schedules.AS400 to UDB – Corporate/Branch AS400 systems as source to STGP01 db2 database in DWDB1 server to separate tables for each source systems.  This is identical replication and Journal Sequence replication. There is no JDE control logic (since JDE SCP runs on weekly basis only) for Daily Load. Only the JDA Control logic is implemented for JDA Daily Load. The Job aborts when U_PROCESS_STATUS =’P’ in JDA Control table for the below 3 tablesEnvironmentSQL Server 2012, Visual Studio-SSIS,SSAS, Winscp,AS400,Data stage,Aqtv9DurationFrom (mm/yy)03/2018To (mm/yy)March/2019Role / ResponsibilitySystem Integration Sr. AnalystContributionsCreating Data Stage links, sessions, Parameter files and workflows using different stages to implement business logic. Preparation of Unit test cases also one of my responsibilities as per the business requirement. And also involved into Unit testing for the stage jobs developed by myself.  Jobs Monitoring0100 PST PM Job (Daily Load-Once/Day-Late Night<--),0300 PST AM Job (0300 - Daily-Every Day-EDM Load Process<--),0600 PST AM Job (Daily Load-Once/Day<--)           DataStage Server 9.1.2 for extraction, loading and transformation (ETL) of data in the data warehouse by applying the transform stage, filter stage, aggregator stage, remove duplicates stage, join stage, lookup stage etc., then we will take the help of DB team to fix it.
